body.title: Git + test discipline — main-only, push-every-close, ticket↔commit linkage
body.statement: Work directly on main — no feature branches while the repo is Akien+CC only (the harness 'branch first' default is wrong here). MAXIM: each commit carries its ticket id, and the ticket carries its final commit hash (overwritten per commit; red stub + green impl both carry the id, no squashing required). Push after EVERY ticket close with the full non-interactive guard: DISPLAY= SSH_ASKPASS_REQUIRE=never GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 GIT_ASKPASS=/bin/true git push origin main — GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 alone is NOT enough; with DISPLAY set, a bad-auth push pops a GUI askpass dialog that hangs ~2min on Akien's screen. Only Claude edits this repo — every regression traces to a prior Claude commit; never hunt for human edits. TEST DISCIPLINE: targeted tests during sprints (changed files + affected tests); the full ~100s suite belongs at day-close or overnight, never repeatedly mid-session; ALWAYS re-run the relevant suite AFTER the final commit, before closing (a clobbered deliverable slips through as 'shipped' otherwise); a discovered pre-existing failure gets its own ticket immediately (priority 0.2 non-blocking, 0.7+ if it blocks). Stash and checkout hazards are law in unseenuniversity/rules/safeguards; mechanics in the builder-gotchas note.
body.why: Unpushed work is invisible to Akien and to the other boxes — 56 commits once piled up locally and drew a sharp correction; the GUI-askpass hang happened twice in one day. The ticket↔hash link is how a closed ticket stays traceable to its code. A red suite has no clean baseline, so every unticketed pre-existing failure hides the next regression.
sources: builder feedback: push-every-ticket (the maxim + guard), commit-to-main-directly, overnight-tests, ticket-preexisting-failures, git-checkout-clobbers-uncommitted (post-commit re-run)
